volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 2.2.624_625b - Section 2.4.702c)

sections.label: Section 2.2.624_625b
sections.translation: What ?— have you brought it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.624_625c
sections.translation: I , myself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.624_625d
sections.translation: Do you at all hesitate to give it me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.626
sections.translation: I , give it you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.626b
sections.original: Mihi hercle vero , qui res rationesque eri
sections.translation: Aye , faith , to me , who manage the business and the accounts of my master Ballio , receive his money , and pay it to him to whom he owes it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.628
sections.original: Si quidem hercle etiam supremi promptas thensauros Iovis ,
sections.translation: By my troth , if you were even the keeper of the treasures of supreme Jove , I would never entrust a groat of silver to you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.629b
sections.translation: While you ’ ve been making yourself so big ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.630
sections.translation: the money has become loose [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.630b
sections.translation: I ’ ll keep it the rather tied up — this way .

sections.label: Section 2.2.631
sections.original: Vae tibi , tu inventu ’ (s e) s vero , meam qui furcilles fidem .
sections.translation: Woe to you ! You indeed have been found to doubt my honor . As though innumerable times as much are not in the habit of being entrusted to me alone .

sections.label: Section 2.2.633
sections.original: Potest ut alii ita arbitrentur et ego ut ne credam tibi .
sections.translation: It ’ s possible that others may think so , and that I mayn ’ t trust you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.634
sections.original: Quasi tu dicas me te velle argento circumducere .
sections.translation: As though you meant to say that I wished to chouse you out of the money .

sections.label: Section 2.2.635
sections.original: Immo vero quasi tu dicas quasique ego autem id suspicer .
sections.translation: Why , yes ; as though you meant to say so ; and as though I , on the other hand , meant to suspect it . But what ’ s your name ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.636b
sections.translation: This procurer has a servant of the name of Syrus ; I ’ ll say that I am he . I am Syrus .

sections.label: Section 2.2.637b
sections.translation: Syrus ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.637c
sections.translation: That ’ s my name .

sections.label: Section 2.2.638
sections.original: Verba multa facimus . erus si tuos domi est , quin provocas ,
sections.translation: We are making many words . If your master ’ s at home , why don ’ t you call him out , that I may transact that for which I was sent here , whatever be your name ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.640
sections.original: Si intus esset , evocarem . verum si dare vis mihi ,
sections.translation: If he were within I would call him out . But if you choose to give it me , it will be more truly paid than if you were to give it to himself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.641b
sections.translation: But now do you know how it is ? My master has sent me to pay this , not to lose it . But I know , to a certainty , that you are in a fever now , because you cannot lay your claws upon it . I shall entrust the money to no person except to Ballio himself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.645
sections.original: At illic nunc negotiosust : res agitur apud iudicem .
sections.translation: But at present he ’ s full of business : a cause of his is being tried before the judge .

sections.label: Section 2.2.646
sections.original: Di bene vortant . at ego quando eum esse censebo domi ,
sections.translation: May the Gods prosper it ! And I , when I shall think that he ’ s at home , will come again . Do you take this letter from me , and give it him : for in it is the token agreed upon between your master and mine about the damsel .

sections.label: Section 2.2.649
sections.original: Scio equidem : ut qui argentum adferret atque expressam imaginem
sections.translation: For my part , I understand it ; the person who should bring the money and the impress of his likeness

sections.label: Section 2.2.650
sections.original: suam huc ad nos , cum eo aiebat velle mitti mulierem .
sections.translation: hither to us , with him he said he wished the damsel to be sent ; for he left a specimen of it here as well .

sections.label: Section 2.2.651b
sections.translation: You understand the whole affair ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.652
sections.translation: Why should I not understand it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.652b
sections.translation: Give him this token then .

sections.label: Section 2.2.652c
sections.translation: Very well . But what ’ s your name ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.653b
sections.translation: Harpax .

sections.label: Section 2.2.653c
sections.translation: Get along with you , Harpax , I like you not . By my troth , you really shan ’ t enter this house , lest you should be doing something in the harpy line [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.655
sections.original: Hostis vivos rapere soleo ex acie : eo hoc nomen mihi est .
sections.translation: I am wont to carry off my enemies alive from the battle-field ; from that circumrstance is my name .

sections.label: Section 2.2.656
sections.original: Pol te multo magis opinor vasa ahena ex aedibus .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I think that you are much more likely to carry off the brass pots from a house .

sections.label: Section 2.2.657
sections.original: Non ita est . sed scin quid te oro , Sure ?
sections.translation: Such is not the fact . But , Syrus , do you know what I request of you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.657b
sections.translation: I shall know , if you tell me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.658
sections.original: Ego devortor extra portam huc in tabernam tertiam ,
sections.translation: I shall put up outside of the gate here , at the third shop , at the house of that tun-bellied , limping , fat old woman , Chrysis .

sections.label: Section 2.2.660
sections.translation: What do you wish then ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.660b
sections.original: Inde ut me arcessas , erus tuos ubi venerit .
sections.translation: That you ’ ll fetch me thence when your master comes .

sections.label: Section 2.2.661
sections.translation: At your pleasure ; by all means .

sections.label: Section 2.2.661b
sections.original: Nam ut lassus veni de via ,
sections.translation: For , as I ’ ve come wearied off my journey , I wish to refresh myself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.662b
sections.translation: You are very wise , and your plan is agreable to me . But take care , please , that you are not out of the way when I send for you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.664
sections.original: Quin ubi prandero , dabo operam somno .
sections.translation: Why , when I ’ ve dined , I shall indulge myself with a nap .

sections.label: Section 2.2.664b
sections.translation: I quite agree with you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.665
sections.translation: Do you wish aught else ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.665b
sections.translation: That you ’ ll be off to take your nap .

sections.label: Section 2.2.665c
sections.translation: I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 2.2.665d
sections.translation: And , do you hear , Harpax ? Order yourself to be covered up , please ; you ’ ll receive the benefit if you take a good sweat .

sections.label: Section 2.3.667
sections.original: di immortales , conservavit me illic homo adventu suo .
sections.translation: Immortal Gods ! this man has preserved me by his coming . By his supply for my journey he has brought me from my wanderings quite into the right way . For the Goddess Opportunity herself could not come to me more opportunely ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.670
sections.original: advenire quam haec allatast mi opportune epistula .
sections.translation: than has this letter in this opportune manner been brought to me . For this has been brought as a horn of plenty [*] , in which there is whatever I wish for : here are my wiles , here all my tricks , here my stratagems , here my money , here his mistress for my master ’ s son so much in love . And now how vaunting shall I show myself ; how , with a breast so fertile in expedients ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.675
sections.original: quo modo quícque agerem , ut lenoni surruperem mulierculam ,
sections.translation: I was to do each thing , how , to steal away the damsel from the procurer , I had all my plans arranged in order in my mind as I desired , fixed , planned out . But , no doubt , thus will it come to pass : this Goddess Fortune , unaided , prevails over the designs of a hundred armed men . And this is the fact , just as each person uses his fortune , so does he surpass others ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.680
sections.original: ita praecellet atque exinde sapere eum omnes dicimus .
sections.translation: and forthwith we all pronounce him wise . When we learn that the counsels of any person have turned out well , we declare that he is a prudent man ; but that he is a fool who is unsuccessful . In our folly we know not how much we are mistaken , when we eagerly wish anything to be given to us ; as though we ourselves could possibly know what is for our advantage .

sections.label: Section 2.3.685
sections.original: certa mittimus , dum incerta petimus ; atque hoc evenit
sections.translation: We lose what is certain , while we are seeking what is uncertain . And this comes to pass , amid labours and amid sorrow , that death meanwhile comes creeping on . But there ’ s enough now of philosophizing ; I have been talking too long , and at too great length . Immortal Gods ! my lie was not dear at its weight in double-distilled gold , which I just now trumped up here on the spur of the moment ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.690
sections.original: quia lenonis me esse dixi . nunc ego hac epistula
sections.translation: when I said that I belonged to the procurer . Now , through this letter shall I deceive three persons — my master , and the procurer , and him who gave me this letter . Excellent ! another thing as well has happened , that I wished for : see , Calidorus is coming ; he is bringing some one with him , I know not whom .

sections.label: Section 2.4.694
sections.original: Dulcia atque amara apud te sum elocutus omnia :
sections.translation: The sweets and the bitters , all have I disclosed to you . You know my love , you know my difficulty , you know my poverty .

sections.label: Section 2.4.696
sections.original: Commemini omnia : id tu modo , me quid vis facere , fac sciam .
sections.translation: I well remember all ; do you only let me know what you want me to do .

sections.label: Section 2.4.697
sections.original: Pseudolus mi ita imperavit , ut aliquem hominem strenuom
sections.translation: Pseudolus has directed me thus , that I should bring to him some bold and zealous person .

sections.label: Section 2.4.698b
sections.translation: You observe your directions well ; for you bring him one both friendly and zealous . But this Pseudolus

sections.label: Section 2.4.700
sections.translation: is a stranger to me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.700b
sections.original: Nimium est mortalis graphicus , † heuretes mihi est .
sections.translation: He is a very clever fellow — he is my contriver . He said that he would effect those things for me that I have told you of .

sections.label: Section 2.4.702
sections.translation: I ’ ll address this person in a very lofty strain .

sections.label: Section 2.4.702b
sections.translation: Whose voice is it that ’ s heard here ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.702c
sections.translation: Oh ! thee , sovereign lord , thee , oh ! thee do I address who dost rule over Pseudolus : thee do I seek , to impart to thee delights thrice three , threefold , acquired by three contrivances ,
